name: 10 - Realistic Operational Health & SLA
description: Analyst questions an operations manager would ask: SLA breach counts and rates, first-response performance, backlog and aging, manager-review queue, reopen and escalation patterns, related-entity counts (comments/attachments per ticket). Every question has a clear single answer and a ExpectedSql aligned with the Tickets schema's SLA + escalation fields.

Scenarios.Question: How many tickets are currently open?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS Total FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON s.Id = t.StatusId WHERE s.IsClosedState = 0 AND t.IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: How many tickets are closed?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS Total FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON s.Id = t.StatusId WHERE s.IsClosedState = 1 AND t.IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: How many tickets have breached SLA?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS Total FROM Tickets WHERE IsSlaBreached = 1 AND IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: What percentage of tickets have breached SLA?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT 100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN IsSlaBreached = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(*),0) AS PctBreached FROM Tickets WHERE IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: Show SLA breach count per priority.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT p.Name AS Priority, COUNT(*) AS BreachedCount FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketPriorities p ON p.Id = t.PriorityId WHERE t.IsSlaBreached = 1 AND t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY p.Name ORDER BY BreachedCount DESC

Scenarios.Question: How many open tickets have not received a first response yet?
Scenarios.Category: IS NULL on date column + status filter
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS Total FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON s.Id = t.StatusId WHERE t.FirstRespondedAt IS NULL AND s.IsClosedState = 0 AND t.IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: How many tickets have been escalated?
Scenarios.Category: IS NOT NULL on date column
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS Total FROM Tickets WHERE EscalatedAt IS NOT NULL AND IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: List tickets that require manager review.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT TicketNumber, Title FROM Tickets WHERE RequiresManagerReview = 1 AND IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: List pending tickets along with their pending reason.
Scenarios.Category: Filter by status with extra column
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.PendingReason FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON s.Id = t.StatusId WHERE s.Name = 'Pending' AND t.IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: Show the top 10 tickets by number of comments.
Scenarios.Category: TOP N with COUNT on related table
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT TOP 10 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, COUNT(c.Id) AS CommentCount FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketComments c ON c.TicketId = t.Id WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY t.TicketNumber, t.Title ORDER BY CommentCount DESC

Scenarios.Question: How many ticket attachments exist?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS Total FROM TicketAttachments

Scenarios.Question: How many tickets have at least one attachment?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT a.TicketId) AS Total FROM TicketAttachments a JOIN Tickets t ON t.Id = a.TicketId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: List tickets that have no comments.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM TicketComments c WHERE c.TicketId = t.Id) AND t.IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: Show all child tickets and their parent ticket id.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT TicketNumber, Title, ParentTicketId FROM Tickets WHERE ParentTicketId IS NOT NULL AND IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: Show ticket counts grouped by escalation level.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT EscalationLevel, COUNT(*) AS Cnt FROM Tickets WHERE IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY EscalationLevel ORDER BY EscalationLevel

Scenarios.Question: How many open tickets are older than 7 days?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS Total FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON s.Id = t.StatusId WHERE s.IsClosedState = 0 AND t.CreatedAt < DATEADD(day,-7,CAST(GETDATE() AS DATE)) AND t.IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: Show the resolution rate per priority as a percentage.
Scenarios.Category: Per-group ratio via SUM(CASE)/COUNT + GROUP BY
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT p.Name AS Priority, 100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN t.ResolvedAt IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(*),0) AS ResolutionRatePct FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketPriorities p ON p.Id = t.PriorityId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY p.Name ORDER BY ResolutionRatePct DESC
